steps.title: Use the study result
steps.content: The blank must follow from the researchers' finding. They found that effort expectancy, meaning perceived ease of use, positively influences users' intention to adopt premium music-streaming services. A logical completion should therefore connect perceived ease with a practical implication for encouraging premium adoption.

steps.title: Reject unsupported shifts
steps.content: Choice B shifts from adoption of premium services to loyalty to Spotify, which the study did not measure. Choice C says other factors probably do not affect users' intentions, but the study only shows that effort expectancy matters. Choice D attacks the reliability of the researchers' measure, which the passage does not support.

steps.title: Apply the implication
steps.content: If perceived ease of use helps drive adoption, then music-streaming companies could reasonably try to influence whether potential users see the premium version as effortless. That follows directly from the positive relationship the researchers found. The answer is A.
